type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Gross electricity production decreased 23% from 2014 values. In 2015, the cogeneration unit operated a
total of 6545 hours for an uptime of 74% as compared to 95% for 2014. Extented shutdown in 2015.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Gross electricity production decreased 23% from 2014 values. In 2015, the cogeneration unit operated a

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The overall emission intensity for 2014 has decreased as compared to 2013 because in the operating
months October – November 2014, the amount of NOx measured by the CEMS remained low. This is
due to the Hot Gas Path Work performed on the Co-generation unit during the September turnaround,
resulting in the turbine operation being cleaner and more efficient. Permit specs changed for emission factor for auxiliary boilers natural gas and tail gas.

Nox emissions were taken from 2014 Annual Report for NOx Emission Trading
Required by Section 55 of Alberta Regulation 33/2006, from Blue Source.

Fuel inventory volumes come from Alberta Specified Gas Emissions, 8th Compliance Report, Reporting Year: 2014 from Blue Source Canada.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The overall emission intensity for 2014 has decreased as compared to 2013 because in the operating
months October – November 2014, the amount of NOx measured by the CEMS remained low. This is
due to the Hot Gas Path Work performed on the Co-generation unit during the September turnaround,
resulting in the turbine operation being cleaner and more efficient. Also,  in 2013 permit specs were used for aux boilers, now we use actual volumes and emission factor.

CO emissions rates were taken from 2014 RATA tests for ATS.

Fuel inventory volumes come from Alberta Specified Gas Emissions, 8th Compliance Report, Reporting Year: 2014 from Blue Source Canada.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: For 2016, the quantity reported to NPRI was only based on the emissions from the gas turbine (by using CEM results). The emissions from other sources (auxiliary boiler, emergency generator, heaters) were not considered in the 2016 report, but were considered for teh 2017 report. A revision to the 2016 NPRI report will be made.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: In previous years, emissions from the auxiliary boiler were estimated by using the permitted value of 0,201 tons per day, which reflected emissions at the nominal calacity of the boiler. For 2017, the estimation method was modified to use instead the AP-42 emission factor, by considering the real consumption of natural gas, as the boiler does not operate continuously at, or near its nominal capacity.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The facility replaced their gas turbine and the cogen unit was not in operation from April 2 - June 10, 2023. This resulted in a decrease in fuel gas usage.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The calculations were updated to separate Genset emissions quantifications from the general NG combustion equipment at the facility.  Originally, the Genset NG usage was included in the "Misc Heaters" category and was using the EPA AP-42 Chapter 1.4 emission factors for natural gas combustion equipment. The emission factors were updated to use EPA AP-42 Chapter 3.2 for natural gas-fired reciprocating engines and equipment-specific emission factors.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The facility replaced their gas turbine and the cogen unit was not in operation from April 2 - June 10, 2023. This resulted in a decrease in fuel gas usage.

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment: Our Cogeneration plant has been running since 2000 with the same gas turbine. To change to a different process/technology will require significant capital and existing permit obligations.
